title: File Servers, Object Storage, CDNs, and Architecture Selection · Go Semantics Case: Ownership Lifecycle
summary: A product sends every byte through one web process, stores uploads on an ephemeral disk, and adds a CDN without deciding authority, mutation, privacy, or failure boundaries. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ownership lifecycle increment in architecture and responsibility decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Assign creation, quarantine, scan, publish, replace, archive, restore, revoke, and delete ownership with terminal states.
Choose server upload, direct upload, redirect, proxy, signed edge delivery, or public delivery from threat, transformation, audit, and performance evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Ownership Lifecycle · Theory Ownership Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 1191 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to select a file, object, gateway, direct, or CDN path from trust and volume evidence; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Assign creation, quarantine, scan, publish, replace, archive, restore, revoke, and delete ownership with terminal states.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Ownership Lifecycle contract for the supplied data.
